journal_entry.reason: Merchandise purchase on account capitalizes the purchase price plus freight-in and shipping insurance to Inventory. Post-acquisition storage and outbound delivery are expensed as period costs. Cash is reduced by all cash payments.

output_debit_classifier.reason:
Target purchased merchandise inventory on account and paid for various freight and storage costs. Under inventory accounting principles, costs necessary to bring inventory to its intended location and condition are capitalized as part of inventory cost (freight-in, shipping insurance). Post-acquisition storage and outbound delivery are period costs expensed as incurred. 

The merchandise purchase of $1,000,000 increases Inventory (asset) and Accounts Payable (liability). The freight to warehouse ($120,000) and shipping insurance ($30,000) are necessary to get the inventory to the warehouse in saleable condition, so they are capitalized to Inventory. The post-acquisition warehouse storage ($20,000) is a period cost expensed as Storage Expense or Warehousing Expense. The outbound delivery freight ($50,000) is a selling expense (Freight-Out or Delivery Expense). All cash payments decrease Cash.

Total debits: Inventory $1,150,000 + Storage Expense $20,000 + Freight-Out Expense $50,000 = $1,220,000
Total credits: Accounts Payable $1,000,000 + Cash $220,000 = $1,220,000
